notes: Career Changers Matrix and Career Starters Matrix files do not exist in O*NET 30.2. No rules written for raw.onet_career_changers or raw.onet_career_starters per domain-context.md recommendation.

rules.name: Occupations: SOC code format XX-XXXX.XX
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM raw.onet_occupations WHERE NOT regexp_matches(onet_soc_code, '^\d{2}-\d{4}\.\d{2}$')
rules.description: Every onet_soc_code must match the O*NET-SOC format XX-XXXX.XX (two digits, hyphen, four digits, period, two digits). Deviation indicates a parsing or coercion failure in the ingestor.
rules.evidence: EDA: 'Format Validation: 100% match XX-XXXX.XX pattern (0 invalid)' across all 1,016 rows. Domain context: 'O*NET-SOC code format: XX-XXXX.XX -- Every onet_soc_code must match.' Threshold: 0 violations (100% conformance).

rules.name: Occupations: onet_soc_code not null
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM raw.onet_occupations WHERE onet_soc_code IS NULL
rules.description: onet_soc_code is the grain field and primary key. Must be present in every row.
rules.evidence: EDA: 'Null Rate: 0% (0 of 1,016)' for O*NET-SOC Code. Schema: onet_soc_code is required=True. Threshold: 0 violations.

rules.name: Occupations: title not null
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM raw.onet_occupations WHERE title IS NULL
rules.description: Every occupation must have a title. Required field per schema.
rules.evidence: EDA: 'Null Rate: 0% (0 of 1,016)' for Title. Schema: title is required=True. Threshold: 0 violations.

rules.name: Occupations: description not null
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM raw.onet_occupations WHERE description IS NULL
rules.description: Every occupation must have a description. Required field per schema.
rules.evidence: EDA: 'Null Rate: 0% (0 of 1,016)' for Description. Schema: description is required=True. Threshold: 0 violations.

rules.name: Occupations: grain uniqueness (onet_soc_code)
rules.sql: SELECT onet_soc_code, COUNT(*) AS cnt FROM raw.onet_occupations GROUP BY onet_soc_code HAVING cnt > 1
rules.description: The declared grain is one row per O*NET-SOC code. Any duplicate indicates an ingestor dedup failure.
rules.evidence: EDA: 'Cardinality: 1,016 distinct values (100% unique)' across 1,016 rows. Zero duplicates. Threshold: 0 duplicates.

rules.name: Occupations: row count 900-1,100
rules.sql: SELECT CASE WHEN cnt < 900 OR cnt > 1100 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END AS violation FROM (SELECT COUNT(*) AS cnt FROM raw.onet_occupations)
rules.description: O*NET 30.2 has 1,016 occupations. Range allows roughly 10% variation across quarterly releases. A count outside this range suggests a download failure or source format change.
rules.evidence: EDA: 'Record Count: 1,016'. Spec estimated ~886 but actual is 1,016 (14.7% higher). EDA recommendation: '1,016 +/- 5%'. Using wider 900-1,100 range to accommodate future releases. Threshold: count in [900, 1100].

rules.name: Occupations: freshness (load_date within 30 days)
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM raw.onet_occupations WHERE load_date < CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '30' DAY LIMIT 1
rules.description: The load_date should be recent (within 30 days of query execution). Stale data suggests the pipeline has not been refreshed.
rules.evidence: Consistent with existing College Scorecard (RAW-CS-017) and BLS OOH (RAW-OOH-017) freshness rules. All rows share the same load_date from a single batch load. Threshold: 0 violations within 30-day window.

rules.name: Task Statements: SOC code format XX-XXXX.XX
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM raw.onet_task_statements WHERE NOT regexp_matches(onet_soc_code, '^\d{2}-\d{4}\.\d{2}$')
rules.description: Every onet_soc_code in Task Statements must match O*NET-SOC format XX-XXXX.XX.
rules.evidence: EDA: 'Format Validation: 100% valid XX-XXXX.XX' across 18,796 rows. 0 invalid. Threshold: 0 violations.

rules.name: Task Statements: task_id not null
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM raw.onet_task_statements WHERE task_id IS NULL
rules.description: task_id is part of the grain (onet_soc_code x task_id). Must be present in every row.
rules.evidence: EDA: 'Null Rate: 0%' for Task ID. Schema: task_id is required=True (LongType). EDA confirms 18,796 distinct task_ids (100% globally unique). Threshold: 0 violations.

rules.name: Task Statements: task not null and not empty
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM raw.onet_task_statements WHERE task IS NULL OR TRIM(task) = ''
rules.description: Every task statement must have a non-empty task description text. This is the core content of the table.
rules.evidence: EDA: 'Null Rate: 0%' for Task. 'Length: min=13, max=332, mean=98.0' -- no empty strings. Schema: task is required=True. Threshold: 0 violations.

rules.name: Task Statements: task_type in valid set or null
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM raw.onet_task_statements WHERE task_type IS NOT NULL AND task_type NOT IN ('Core', 'Supplemental', 'n/a')
rules.description: task_type must be one of 'Core', 'Supplemental', or 'n/a' (analyst-derived tasks). The 'n/a' value correlates 1:1 with domain_source='Analyst' (845 rows, 4.5%).
rules.evidence: EDA: 'Distribution: Core: 13,643 (72.6%), Supplemental: 4,308 (22.9%), n/a: 845 (4.5%).' Only these 3 values observed. 'n/a' correlates exactly with domain_source='Analyst'. Threshold: 0 violations.

rules.name: Task Statements: referential integrity to onet_occupations
rules.sql: SELECT DISTINCT t.onet_soc_code FROM raw.onet_task_statements t LEFT JOIN raw.onet_occupations o ON t.onet_soc_code = o.onet_soc_code WHERE o.onet_soc_code IS NULL
rules.description: Every onet_soc_code in Task Statements must exist in the Occupation Data master table. Orphan SOC codes indicate a data integrity failure.
rules.evidence: EDA: 'Referential Integrity: All 923 SOC codes exist in Occupation Data.' 0 orphans. Threshold: 0 orphan SOC codes.

rules.name: Task Statements: row count 18,000-22,000
rules.sql: SELECT CASE WHEN cnt < 18000 OR cnt > 22000 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END AS violation FROM (SELECT COUNT(*) AS cnt FROM raw.onet_task_statements)
rules.description: O*NET 30.2 has 18,796 task statements. Task lists evolve with rolling updates so a wider range is appropriate.
rules.evidence: EDA: 'Record Count: 18,796'. EDA recommendation: '18,796 +/- 10%' which gives 16,916-20,676. Using rounded 18,000-22,000 for cleaner bounds and forward compatibility. Threshold: count in [18000, 22000].

rules.name: Work Activities: SOC code format XX-XXXX.XX
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM raw.onet_work_activities WHERE NOT regexp_matches(onet_soc_code, '^\d{2}-\d{4}\.\d{2}$')
rules.description: Every onet_soc_code in Work Activities must match O*NET-SOC format XX-XXXX.XX.
rules.evidence: EDA: 'Format Validation: 100% valid' across 73,308 rows. Threshold: 0 violations.

rules.name: Work Activities: grain uniqueness (onet_soc_code x element_id x scale_id)
rules.sql: SELECT onet_soc_code, element_id, scale_id, COUNT(*) AS cnt FROM raw.onet_work_activities GROUP BY onet_soc_code, element_id, scale_id HAVING cnt > 1
rules.description: The declared grain is one row per occupation x activity x scale. Any duplicate indicates an ingestor dedup failure.
rules.evidence: EDA: 'Grain: O*NET-SOC Code x Element ID x Scale ID (verified unique -- 0 duplicates)' across 73,308 rows. Threshold: 0 duplicates.

rules.name: Work Activities: IM scale data_value range 1.0-5.0
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM raw.onet_work_activities WHERE scale_id = 'IM' AND (data_value < 1.0 OR data_value > 5.0)
rules.description: Importance (IM) scale ratings must be in the range [1.0, 5.0] per O*NET Scales Reference. Values outside this range indicate data corruption or parsing failure.
rules.evidence: EDA: 'IM scale: min=1.00, max=4.99'. O*NET Scales Reference defines IM range as 1-5. Domain context: 'IM = Importance (1-5)'. All 36,654 IM rows are within range. Threshold: 0 violations.

rules.name: Work Activities: LV scale data_value range 0.0-7.0
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM raw.onet_work_activities WHERE scale_id = 'LV' AND (data_value < 0.0 OR data_value > 7.0)
rules.description: Level (LV) scale ratings must be in the range [0.0, 7.0] per O*NET Scales Reference. 0.0 indicates 'not relevant'. Values outside this range indicate data corruption.
rules.evidence: EDA: 'LV scale: min=0.00, max=6.81'. O*NET Scales Reference defines LV range as 0-7. Domain context: 'LV = Level (0-7)'. All 36,654 LV rows are within range. Threshold: 0 violations.

rules.name: Work Activities: scale_id in valid set
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM raw.onet_work_activities WHERE scale_id NOT IN ('IM', 'LV')
rules.description: Work Activities uses exactly two scales: IM (Importance) and LV (Level). Any other scale_id indicates data contamination or source format change.
rules.evidence: EDA: 'Distribution: IM: 36,654 (50.0%), LV: 36,654 (50.0%)'. Only these 2 values. Perfectly balanced. Threshold: 0 violations.

rules.name: Work Activities: recommend_suppress in valid set
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM raw.onet_work_activities WHERE recommend_suppress IS NOT NULL AND recommend_suppress NOT IN ('Y', 'N', 'n/a')
rules.description: recommend_suppress must be 'Y' (suppress recommended), 'N' (reliable), or 'n/a' (not applicable for expert/analyst sources). O*NET uses this as its primary data quality signal.
rules.evidence: EDA: 'Distribution: N: 54,943 (74.9%), n/a: 17,302 (23.6%), Y: 1,063 (1.5%).' Only these 3 values observed. Threshold: 0 violations.

rules.name: Work Activities: referential integrity to onet_occupations
rules.sql: SELECT DISTINCT wa.onet_soc_code FROM raw.onet_work_activities wa LEFT JOIN raw.onet_occupations o ON wa.onet_soc_code = o.onet_soc_code WHERE o.onet_soc_code IS NULL
rules.description: Every onet_soc_code in Work Activities must exist in the Occupation Data master table.
rules.evidence: EDA: 'Referential Integrity: All 894 exist in Occupation Data.' 0 orphans. Threshold: 0 orphan SOC codes.

rules.name: Work Activities: row count 70,000-80,000
rules.sql: SELECT CASE WHEN cnt < 70000 OR cnt > 80000 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END AS violation FROM (SELECT COUNT(*) AS cnt FROM raw.onet_work_activities)
rules.description: O*NET 30.2 has 73,308 work activity rows (894 occupations x 41 activities x 2 scales = 73,308). Row count is very stable as it is a product of fixed structure.
rules.evidence: EDA: 'Record Count: 73,308'. EDA recommendation: '73,308 +/- 5%' (69,643-76,973). Using rounded 70,000-80,000 for cleaner bounds. Threshold: count in [70000, 80000].

rules.name: Work Context: SOC code format XX-XXXX.XX
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM raw.onet_work_context WHERE NOT regexp_matches(onet_soc_code, '^\d{2}-\d{4}\.\d{2}$')
rules.description: Every onet_soc_code in Work Context must match O*NET-SOC format XX-XXXX.XX.
rules.evidence: EDA: 'Format Validation: 100% valid' across 297,676 rows (894 distinct SOC codes). Threshold: 0 violations.

rules.name: Work Context: grain uniqueness (onet_soc_code x element_id x scale_id x category)
rules.sql: SELECT onet_soc_code, element_id, scale_id, category, COUNT(*) AS cnt FROM raw.onet_work_context GROUP BY onet_soc_code, element_id, scale_id, category HAVING cnt > 1
rules.description: The grain includes category because CXP/CTP scales produce multiple rows per element (one per response category). The composite key onet_soc_code x element_id x scale_id x category must be unique. Note: category is NULL for CX/CT point-estimate rows.
rules.evidence: EDA: 'Grain: O*NET-SOC Code x Element ID x Scale ID x Category (verified unique -- 0 duplicates)' across 297,676 rows. Threshold: 0 duplicates.

rules.name: Work Context: scale_id in valid set
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM raw.onet_work_context WHERE scale_id NOT IN ('CX', 'CT', 'CXP', 'CTP')
rules.description: Work Context uses exactly four scales: CX (context 5-point), CT (context 3-point), CXP (context 5-category percentage), CTP (context 3-category percentage). Any other value indicates source format change.
rules.evidence: EDA: 'Distribution: CXP: 241,450 (81.1%), CX: 49,170 (16.5%), CTP: 5,268 (1.8%), CT: 1,788 (0.6%).' Only these 4 values. Threshold: 0 violations.

rules.name: Work Context: CX data_value range 1.0-5.0
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM raw.onet_work_context WHERE scale_id = 'CX' AND (data_value < 1.0 OR data_value > 5.0)
rules.description: CX (context point estimate) scale ratings must be in [1.0, 5.0]. These are single-value ratings for 5-point context dimensions.
rules.evidence: EDA: 'CX: min=1.00, max=5.00, mean=2.79.' O*NET Scales Reference defines CX range as 1-5. Threshold: 0 violations.

rules.name: Work Context: CT data_value range 1.0-7.0
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM raw.onet_work_context WHERE scale_id = 'CT' AND (data_value < 1.0 OR data_value > 7.0)
rules.description: CT (context 3-point estimate) scale ratings must be in [1.0, 7.0]. EDA observed range 1.00-3.00 for the 2 current CT elements, but the O*NET scale system allows up to 7.0 for context scales. Using the wider domain range for forward compatibility.
rules.evidence: EDA: 'CT: min=1.00, max=3.00.' Current CT elements (Work Schedules, Duration of Typical Work Week) use 3-point scales, but the CT scale type in the O*NET system supports up to 7 points. Domain context: 'CT scale range 1-7.' Using [1.0, 7.0] as the domain-safe range. Threshold: 0 violations.

rules.name: Work Context: CXP/CTP data_value range 0.0-100.0
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM raw.onet_work_context WHERE scale_id IN ('CXP', 'CTP') AND (data_value < 0.0 OR data_value > 100.0)
rules.description: CXP and CTP are percentage scales representing the proportion of respondents choosing each response category. Values must be in [0.0, 100.0].
rules.evidence: EDA: 'CXP: min=0.00, max=100.00. CTP: min=0.00, max=100.00.' Both are percentage values. CXP mean=20.00 (expected: 100/5 categories). CTP mean=33.33 (expected: 100/3 categories). Threshold: 0 violations.

rules.name: Work Context: category populated for CXP/CTP scales
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM raw.onet_work_context WHERE scale_id IN ('CXP', 'CTP') AND category IS NULL
rules.description: CXP and CTP rows represent specific response categories and must have a category value (1-5 for CXP, 1-3 for CTP). A null category on a percentage row means the category-percentage structure is broken.
rules.evidence: EDA: 'Category distribution: 1: 50,046, 2: 50,046, 3: 50,046, 4: 48,290, 5: 48,290, n/a: 50,958.' The n/a values (coerced to NULL by ingestor) are on CX/CT point-estimate rows only. All CXP/CTP rows have valid category values. Threshold: 0 violations.

rules.name: Work Context: referential integrity to onet_occupations
rules.sql: SELECT DISTINCT wc.onet_soc_code FROM raw.onet_work_context wc LEFT JOIN raw.onet_occupations o ON wc.onet_soc_code = o.onet_soc_code WHERE o.onet_soc_code IS NULL
rules.description: Every onet_soc_code in Work Context must exist in the Occupation Data master table.
rules.evidence: EDA: 'Referential Integrity: All 894 exist in Occupation Data. Same 122 missing as Work Activities.' 0 orphans. Threshold: 0 orphan SOC codes.

rules.name: Work Context: row count 280,000-320,000
rules.sql: SELECT CASE WHEN cnt < 280000 OR cnt > 320000 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END AS violation FROM (SELECT COUNT(*) AS cnt FROM raw.onet_work_context)
rules.description: O*NET 30.2 has 297,676 work context rows. This is 6x the original spec estimate due to CXP/CTP category-percentage rows. Row count is relatively stable as it is driven by the fixed 57-element taxonomy.
rules.evidence: EDA: 'Record Count: 297,676'. EDA recommendation: '297,676 +/- 5%' (282,792-312,560). Using rounded 280,000-320,000 for cleaner bounds. Threshold: count in [280000, 320000].

rules.name: Related Occupations: source SOC code format XX-XXXX.XX
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM raw.onet_related_occupations WHERE NOT regexp_matches(onet_soc_code, '^\d{2}-\d{4}\.\d{2}$')
rules.description: Every source onet_soc_code must match O*NET-SOC format XX-XXXX.XX.
rules.evidence: EDA: 'Format Validation: 100% valid XX-XXXX.XX' for source SOC (923 distinct). Threshold: 0 violations.

rules.name: Related Occupations: related SOC code format XX-XXXX.XX
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM raw.onet_related_occupations WHERE NOT regexp_matches(related_onet_soc_code, '^\d{2}-\d{4}\.\d{2}$')
rules.description: Every related_onet_soc_code must match O*NET-SOC format XX-XXXX.XX.
rules.evidence: EDA: 'Format Validation: 100% valid XX-XXXX.XX' for related SOC (920 distinct). Threshold: 0 violations.

rules.name: Related Occupations: grain uniqueness (onet_soc_code x related_onet_soc_code)
rules.sql: SELECT onet_soc_code, related_onet_soc_code, COUNT(*) AS cnt FROM raw.onet_related_occupations GROUP BY onet_soc_code, related_onet_soc_code HAVING cnt > 1
rules.description: The declared grain is one row per source-related occupation pair. Any duplicate indicates an ingestor dedup failure.
rules.evidence: EDA: 'Grain: O*NET-SOC Code x Related O*NET-SOC Code (verified unique -- 0 duplicates)' across 18,460 rows. Threshold: 0 duplicates.

rules.name: Related Occupations: related_index range 1-20
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM raw.onet_related_occupations WHERE related_index < 1 OR related_index > 20
rules.description: Related index must be in [1, 20]. Index 1-5 = Primary-Short, 6-10 = Primary-Long, 11-20 = Supplemental. Values outside this range indicate data corruption.
rules.evidence: EDA: 'Range: 1-20. Exactly 923 rows per index value (perfectly uniform).' 0 values outside range. Threshold: 0 violations.

rules.name: Related Occupations: is_primary consistent with index
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM raw.onet_related_occupations WHERE (related_index <= 10 AND is_primary = false) OR (related_index > 10 AND is_primary = true)
rules.description: is_primary must be true for index 1-10 (Primary-Short and Primary-Long) and false for index 11-20 (Supplemental). This is derived by the ingestor from the Relatedness Tier column.
rules.evidence: EDA: 'The is_primary derivation in the ingestor (index <= 10 = primary) is approximately correct' and 'Relatedness Tier: Primary-Short (index 1-5), Primary-Long (index 6-10), Supplemental (index 11-20).' Both Primary-Short and Primary-Long map to is_primary=true. Threshold: 0 violations.

rules.name: Related Occupations: no self-references
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM raw.onet_related_occupations WHERE onet_soc_code = related_onet_soc_code
rules.description: An occupation must not be listed as related to itself. Self-references are domain-impossible.
rules.evidence: EDA: 'No self-references: 0 cases where source SOC = related SOC.' Threshold: 0 violations.

rules.name: Related Occupations: referential integrity (source SOC)
rules.sql: SELECT DISTINCT ro.onet_soc_code FROM raw.onet_related_occupations ro LEFT JOIN raw.onet_occupations o ON ro.onet_soc_code = o.onet_soc_code WHERE o.onet_soc_code IS NULL
rules.description: Every source onet_soc_code in Related Occupations must exist in the Occupation Data master table.
rules.evidence: EDA: 'Referential Integrity: All 923 exist in Occupation Data' for source SOC. Threshold: 0 orphans.

rules.name: Related Occupations: referential integrity (related SOC)
rules.sql: SELECT DISTINCT ro.related_onet_soc_code FROM raw.onet_related_occupations ro LEFT JOIN raw.onet_occupations o ON ro.related_onet_soc_code = o.onet_soc_code WHERE o.onet_soc_code IS NULL
rules.description: Every related_onet_soc_code must also exist in the Occupation Data master table. This ensures related occupations are not orphan references.
rules.evidence: EDA: 'Referential Integrity: All 920 [related SOC codes] exist in Occupation Data.' Threshold: 0 orphans.

rules.name: Related Occupations: exactly 20 rows per source occupation
rules.sql: SELECT onet_soc_code, COUNT(*) AS cnt FROM raw.onet_related_occupations GROUP BY onet_soc_code HAVING cnt != 20
rules.description: Every source occupation should have exactly 20 related occupations (10 primary + 10 supplemental). O*NET 30.2 data is perfectly uniform at 20 per occupation.
rules.evidence: EDA: 'Exactly 20 rows per source occupation -- no exceptions. 923 occupations x 20 = 18,460.' 0 occupations with != 20 rows. Threshold: 0 violations.

rules.name: Related Occupations: row count 17,000-20,000
rules.sql: SELECT CASE WHEN cnt < 17000 OR cnt > 20000 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END AS violation FROM (SELECT COUNT(*) AS cnt FROM raw.onet_related_occupations)
rules.description: O*NET 30.2 has 18,460 related occupation rows (923 x 20). Row count scales with the number of occupations surveyed.
rules.evidence: EDA: 'Record Count: 18,460'. EDA recommendation: '18,460 +/- 5%' (17,537-19,383). Using 17,000-20,000 for cleaner bounds and forward compatibility. Threshold: count in [17000, 20000].
